steps.title: Read Ortega's objection
steps.content: Text 2 says Ortega thinks treating Moche portrait vessels as hyper-realistic depictions may overlook the creators' imagination. She argues the vessels could use realistic subjects to explore abstract concepts.

steps.title: Apply it to the markings
steps.content: Ortega would likely say the facial markings are not necessarily literal records of individual physical features. A compares faces with plants and animals, B supports the hyper-realistic reading, and C introduces excavation damage not mentioned in either text. The answer is D.
